title: Lemma D: degenerate cases, conventions (multiple roots, n=1), equality characterization

comments.author: human
comments.body:
Boundary cases that the main argument does not cover:

1. **Multiple-root inputs:** if $p$ has a repeated root then $\Phi_n(p)=+\infty$, so $1/\Phi_n(p)=0$; verify $(\star)$ reduces correctly and the limiting argument (Lemma A) delivers it.
2. **$n=1$:** the score vector is empty, so $\Phi_1\equiv 0$ and $1/\Phi_1=+\infty$. The inequality $1/\Phi_1(p\boxplus_1 q)\ge 1/\Phi_1(p)+1/\Phi_1(q)$ reads $\infty\ge\infty$. Fix the convention and confirm the statement is (vacuously) true; ensure no solver-introduced convention is smuggled in that is absent from the problem statement.
3. **Equality case:** characterize when equality holds (expected: the finite analogue of the Gaussian/semicircle extremizer, e.g. roots in arithmetic progression / Hermite-type polynomials). Not required for the inequality but expected for completeness.
